title: Required reserves, excess reserves, and bank behavior
description: In this exercise, practice calculating key outcomes, reading balance sheets, and drawing conclusions about what banks can do in the expansion of the money supply.
topic_path: Banking and the expansion of the money supply
Required reserves, excess reserves, and bank behavior

assessment_items.question_content:
The central bank of Mordor has a $25\%$ reserve requirement. Rose deposits $\$10,000$ into her local, commercial bank in Mordor.

**How much will required reserves increase as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To calculate the amount of reserves required for each deposit, multiply the required reserve percentage (rr) by the size of the deposit:

$\text{Amount of required reserves} = rr \times \text{deposit}$

If the reserve requirement is $25\%$ and $\$10{,}000$ is deposited, you would use the equation:

$\begin{align}
\text{Amount of reserves}  &= 25\% \times \$10,000\\\\
&=.25 \times \$10,000\end{align}$

If the reserve requirement is $25\%$ and $\$10{,}000$ is deposited, the required reserves are:

$\begin{align} &=0.25 \times \$10{,}000\\\\
&=\$2{,}500\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
Following a large deposit, excess reserves at the Second National Bank of Middle Earth have increased by $\$20{,}000$.

**How much was that large deposit if the reserve requirement in this country is $20\%$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
$\begin{align}\text{Excess reserves} & = \text{deposits} - (\text{deposits} \times  rr)\\\\
&= \text{deposits} \times (1-rr) \end{align}$
Excess reserves have increased by $\$20{,}000$, and the reserve requirement is $20\%$:

$\$20{,}000 = \text{deposits} \times (1- 0.2)$
Solving for the amount of the deposits:

$\begin{align} \$20,000 &= deposits \times (1- 0.2)\\\\
\$20,000 &= deposits \times 0.8\\\\
\$20,000 \div 0.8&=deposits\\\\
\$25,000 &= deposits\end{align}$
The amount deposited was $\$25{,}000$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Burgin National Bank operates under a fractional reserve system and the required reserve ratio is $10\%$. The bank has $\$4{,}000$ in owner’s equity, $\$20{,}000$ in demand deposits, $\$10{,}000$ in business loans, and $\$7{,}000$ in consumer loans.

**Based on this information, how much does Burgin National Bank need to keep in required reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The First Bank of Hamsterville operates under a fractional reserve system and the required reserve ratio is $20\%$. The bank has $\$100{,}000$ in demand deposits, $\$50{,}000$ in business loans, and $\$15{,}000$ in consumer loans.

**Based on this information, how much does First Bank need to keep in required reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Mesa Verde Bank has $\$80{,}000$ in demand deposits, $\$20{,}000$ in bonds, and no owner's equity. It has a reserve requirement of $5\%$.

**How much does Mesa Verde need to keep in required reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

First Bank has a reserve requirement of $25\%$. One of its customers, Gendry, found $\$10{,}000$ buried in a pit inside his blacksmith shop and deposited it into First Bank.

**What happens to First Bank’s required reserves and excess reserves as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Only $25\%$ of a $\$10{,}000$ deposit must be kept in required reserves, and the rest is excess reserves. This means excess reserves change, and required reserves will increase by less than the full $\$10{,}000$ deposit.
Required reserves increase by $\$2{,}500$:

$\$10{,}000 \times 25\% = \$2{,}500$
Excess reserves increase by the difference between the deposit and required reserves.
Excess reserves increase by $\$7{,}500$:


$\$10{,}0000 - \$2{,}500 = \$7{,}500$

choices.content: Required reserves increase $\$2{,}500$, excess reserves increase $\$7{,}500$.
Required reserves don’t change, excess reserves increase $\$10{,}000$.
Required reserves don’t change, excess reserves increase $\$2{,}500$.
Required reserves increase $\$7{,}500$, excess reserves increase $\$2{,}500$.
Required reserves increase $\$10{,}000$, excess reserves don't change.

assessment_items.question_content:
Mesa Verde Bank has $\$80{,}000$ in demand deposits, $\$20{,}000$ in bonds, and no owner's equity. It has a reserve requirement of $5\%$. Kim finds a bag with $\$2{,}000$ in it and deposits it into the bank.

**What happens to Mesa Verde Bank’s required reserves and excess reserves as a result of this deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Only $5\%$ of a $\$2{,}000$ deposit must be kept in required reserves, and the rest is excess reserves. This means excess reserves change, and required reserves will increase by less than the full $\$2{,}000$ deposit.
Required reserves increase by $\$100$:

$\$2{,}000 \times 5\% = \$100$
Excess reserves increase by the difference between the deposit and required reserves.
Excess reserves increase by $\$1{,}900$:


$\$2{,}000 - \$100 = \$1{,}900$

choices.content: Required reserves increase by $\$100$, excess reserves increase $\$1{,}900$.
Required reserves don’t change, excess reserves increase $\$2{,}000$.
Required reserves don’t change, excess reserves increase $\$1{,}000$.
Required reserves increase $\$2{,}000$, excess reserves increase $\$2{,}000$.
Required reserves increase $\$2{,}000$, excess reserves don't change.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Third Bank of Chidi is one of many privately owned banks in Anagonye. It sells $\$200$ worth of government securities that it had previously been holding.

**Which of the following best describes the initial effect of this sale on the bank’s balance sheet ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Its excess reserves increase, but its total assets don't change.
Its required reserves increase, and it's total assets decrease.

assessment_items.question_content:
The current balance sheet for the Third Bank of Westeros is shown below. The reserve requirement in Westeros is 25%.

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-
Reserves: $\$5{,}000$ | Demand deposits: $\$20{,}000$ 
Loans: $\$15{,}000$ | Owners’ equity: $\$0$


**If Ygritte deposits $\$1000$ into her checking account in the Third Bank of Westeros, how much will the bank have in excess reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
In order to determine how much a bank has in reserves, and as a result can make in new loans following a new deposit, follow these steps:

1. Determine if the bank has excess reserves prior to the deposit.

2. Determine how much of the new deposit must be held as required reserves: the remainder of the deposit becomes excess reserves. 

3. Add the excess reserves prior to the new deposit to the excess reserves from the new deposit to get the new excess reserves.
(1) Determine if the bank has excess reserves prior to the deposit:

- The bank has $\$20{,}000$ in demand deposits prior to the new deposit. Its reserve requirement is $25\%$, so it must keep $25\%$ of $\$20{,}000$—$\$5{,}000$—in required reserves.

- The bank currently holds total reserves of $\$5{,}000$, so all of its reserves prior to Ygritte’s deposit are required reserves.
(2) Determine how much of the new deposit must be held as required reserves: the remainder of the deposit becomes excess reserves. 

- The bank must keep $25\%$ of Ygritte’s deposit on reserve; $25\%$ of $\$1{,}000$ is $\$250$.Therefore, $\$250$ of Ygritte’s $\$1{,}000$ is required reserves and $\$750$—the remainder—is excess reserves.
(3) Add the excess reserves prior to the new deposit to the excess reserves from the new deposit to get the new excess reserves.

$\begin{align}\text{Total available for new loan} &=\text{Excess reserves before the deposit} +\text{ Excess reserves after the deposit} \\\\
\$0 + \$750 &= \$750\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The reserve requirement is $20\%$. Excellence Bank’s balance sheet is shown below.

Assets | Liabilities and Equity
-|-
$\begin{align}\text{loans: } &\$50{,}000\\\\ \text{bonds: } &\$10{,}000\\\\ \text{reserves: } &\$20{,}000 \end{align}$| $\text{Deposits: }  \$80{,}000$ 
**Total assets**: $\$80{,}000$ | **Total liabilities**: $\$80{,}000$

**If Jianyu withdraws $\$5{,}000$ from his bank account, which of the following is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The bank has no excess reserves.
The bank is not meeting its reserve requirement.
The bank can make $\$4{,}000$ in additional loans
The bank can make $\$1{,}000$ in additional loans.

assessment_items.question_content:
The balance sheet for Chidi Bank is given below. Assume the reserve requirement is $20\%$

Assets | Liabilities and Equity
-|-
$\begin{align} \text{loans}: &\$30{,}000 \\\\\text{bonds}: &\$10{,}000\\\\\text{reserves}: &\$20{,}000\end{align}$ | Deposits: $\$60{,}000$
Total assets: $\$60{,}000$ | Total liabilities: $\$60{,}000$

**If this bank sells all of the bonds it holds to the central bank, how much does this bank have in excess reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The reserve requirement is $20\%$. Excellence Bank’s balance sheet is shown below.

Assets | Liabilities and Equity
-|-
$\begin{align}\text{loans: } &\$50{,}000\\\\ \text{bonds: } &\$10{,}000\\\\ \text{reserves: } &\$20{,}000 \end{align}$| $\text{Deposits: }  \$80{,}000$ 
**Total assets**: $\$80{,}000$ | **Total liabilities**: $\$80{,}000$

**If this bank sells all of the bonds it holds to the central bank, how much does this bank have in excess reserves?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The following is the balance sheet for Cricetinae Federal Bank which is one of many banks in Hamsterville. The reserve requirement in Hamsterville is $10\%$.

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-
$\begin{align}\text{Loans}: &\$50{,}000\\\\\text{Reserves}: &\$25{,}000\\\\ \text{Government bonds}: &\$20{,}000\\\\ \text{Municipal bonds}: &\$5{,}000\end{align}$   | Demand deposits: $\$100{,}000$
Total assets: $\$100{,}000$ | Total liabilities: $\$100{,}000$

**How much in new loans can this bank make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
A bank can loan out its excess reserves. Excess reserves are the difference between its actual reserves and its required reserves.
This bank has $\$100{,}000$ in deposits, so a 10% reserve requirement means the bank must keep $\$10{,}000$ on hand:

$\begin{align}\text{required reserves} &=\text{reserve requirement} \times \text{demand deposits}\\\\
&= 10\% \times \$100{,}000\\\\
&= \$10{,}000\end{align}$

This bank has $\$25{,}000$ in reserves, so it has $\$15{,}000$ in excess reserves:

$\begin{align} \text{excess reserves} &=\text{total reserves} - \text{required reserves}\\\\
&=\$25{,}000 - \$10{,}000 \\\\
&=\$15{,}000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The First Bank of Cloud City has total reserves of $\$1000$, excess reserves of $\$800$, and required reserves of $\$200$.

**What is the dollar value of new loans that this bank can make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Mitoocents Bank’s balance sheet is shown below. The central bank currently has a reserve requirement of $50\%$.

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-
$\begin{align} \text{Loans}: &\$70{,}000\\\\ \text{Government securities}: &\$10{,}000&\\\\ \text{Total reserves}: &\$80{,}000 \\\\ \end{align}$	|  $\begin{align} \text{Checkable deposits:}&\$120{,}000\\\\\text{Owner’s equity:} &\$40{,}000\end{align}$
Total Assets: $\$160{,}000$ | Total Liabilities & Equity: $\$160{,}000$


**If the central bank lowers the reserve requirement to $25\%$, which of the following is the most likely outcome?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The amount of government bonds held decreased by $\$8{,}000$.

$\text{Total Assets:} \$160{,}000$ | $\text{Total Liabilities & Equity:}\$160{,}000$

**How do excess reserves change if the central bank lowers the reserve requirement from $25\%$ to $20\%$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Bread Bank is one of many banks in Fredland and there is a currently a $10\%$ reserve requirement. Its balance sheet is shown below.

Assets | Liabilities & Equity
-|-
$\begin{align}\text{Loans}: &\$50{,}000\\\\ \text{Reserves}: &\$25{,}000\\\\ \text{Government bonds}: &\$20{,}000\\\\ \text{Municipal bonds}: &\$5{,}000\end{align}$ |  Demand deposits: $\$100{,}000$
Total assets: $\$100{,}000$ | Total liabilities: $\$100{,}000$

**What happens to required reserves and excess reserves if the reserve requirement is increased to $20\%$**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Required reserves increase to $\$20{,}000$; excess reserves decrease to $\$5{,}000$.
Required reserves increase to $\$10{,}000$; excess reserves increase to $\$20{,}000$.
Required reserves decrease to $\$10{,}000$; excess reserves increase to $\$10{,}000$.
Required reserves increase to $\$25{,}000$; excess reserves increase to $\$0$.
Required reserves increase to $\$10{,}000$; excess reserves are unchanged.

assessment_items.question_content:
The central bank of Hamsterville requires banks to keep $20\%$ of deposit accounts on reserve at the central bank. Assume all money is kept in banks and banks keep no excess reserves.

**What is the maximum increase in the money supply that occurs if the monetary base increases by $\$100$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The central bank of Hamsterville requires banks to keep $20\%$ of deposit accounts on reserve at the central bank. Assume all money is kept in banks and banks keep no excess reserves.

**What is the maximum increase in the money supply that occurs if there is a new deposit of $\$100$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: When a deposit is made, a bank must keep some of that and loan the rest. The amount it loans out forms the bases of the multiple expansion of the money supply.

When the reserve requirement is $20\%$, the bank must keep $\$20$ of a $\$100$ deposit on reserve and can loan out the remainder: 

$\$100 - \$20 = \$80$.

The excess reserves are then the basis of the multiple expansion of the money supply:

$\begin{align} \text{maximum increase in money supply} &=\dfrac{1}{RR} \times \text{excess reserves}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{0.2} \times \$80\\\\
&=5\times \$80\\\\
&=\$400\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The reserve requirement in Westeros is $25\%$. Assume that banks keep no excess reserves and there are no other monetary leakages. 

**What is the maximum possible increase in the money supply if an additional $\$500$ is added to the monetary base?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The maximum increase in the monetary supply possible from an increase in the monetary base is the money multiplier times the increase in the monetary base:

$\text{Maximum increase in the money supply} =\dfrac{1}{\text{reserve requirement}} \times \text{increase in the monetary base}$

$\begin{align}\text{Maximum increase in the money supply} &=\dfrac{1}{\text{reserve requirement}} \times \text{increase in the monetary base}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{0.25} \times \$500\\\\
&=4 \times \$500\\\\
&=\$2000\end{align}$

assessment_items.question_content:
The reserve requirement in Westeros is $25\%$. Assume that banks keep no excess reserves and there are no other monetary leakages. 

**What is the increase in the money supply from a new $\$500$ deposit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When the reserve requirement is $25\%$, the bank must keep $\$125$ of a $\$500$ deposit on reserve and can loan out the remainder: 

$\$500 - \$125 = \$375$.

The excess reserves are then the basis of the multiple expansion of the money supply:

$\begin{align} \text{maximum increase in money supply} &=\dfrac{1}{RR} \times \text{excess reserves}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{0.25} \times \$375\\\\
&=4\times \$375\\\\
&=\$1500\end{align}$

**If the monetary base increases by $\$200$ and this leads to an increase in the money supply of $\$1000$, what is the reserve requirement in Susanland?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**If the reserve requirement is $5\%$ and banks keep no excess reserves, what is the money multiplier?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Banks in Hamsterville keep no excess reserves. The reserve requirement in Hamsterville is $10\%$

**What is the money multiplier?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

In Lincolnland the reserve requirement is $20\%$ and banks keep no excess reserves.

**What is the money multiplier?**
